mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that months with positive PAYEMS growth do not exhibit higher 20-day forward returns than months with negative growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.25%, restrictive financial conditions dominate discount rate effects and lead to negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.25%, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive three-month change in consumer prices alongside negative three-month change in industrial production identifies a stagflationary supply contraction that squeezes corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when CPIAUCSL rises and INDPRO falls over a 3-month window, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of observed instances, or the rank correlation between the interaction term and returns is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing cycle, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal accelerating construction activity and durable goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this link breaks outside of easing cycles.
failure_condition: In monetary easing cycles (FEDFUNDS declining over 6 months), positive HOUST changes do not produce statistically higher 20-day forward returns than in non-easing cycles (t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales triggers immediate negative earnings revisions and de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with negative RSAFS growth do not produce lower mean 5-day forward returns than months with positive growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate signals recessionary demand destruction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based demand expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth do not exhibit higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than quarters with negative growth (rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals near-term earnings contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with negative INDPRO growth show nonnegative mean 5-day forward returns, or the frequency of negative returns is below 50%.
